Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Visual Services / Cornell Publications Photography records
Cornell University. Publications Photography
4-3-1885
19 cubic feet.
[ca. 1960-1989] Photographic prints and slides. Slides of Cornell University buildings, classrooms, students, alumni, professors, athletics, and other Cornell and Ithaca, New York subjects and scenes. Photographs, ca. 1960-1980; 35 mm. black and white transparencies of historical campus photographs; 2 x 2 black and white and color slides, some identified; 35 mm. and 2 x 2 negatives, with guide; and contact sheets of prints and negatives; 8 x 10 photographs of the Theory Center; 35 mm. slides of Roberts Hall; 35 mm. slides of Maplewood Park. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 2 ======= 1 2 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2100812
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Noni Korf Vidal
Date completed:

EAD encoding:
Peter M. Martinez, November 2001
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, October 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Office of Visual Services was formed in the early 1960s. By the mid 1980s it was called the Cornell Office of University Relations-Visual Services. Subsequently the name was changed to Cornell University Office of Publications Services, and then to the Office of Cornell Publications.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Slides of Cornell University buildings, classrooms, students, alumni, professors, athletics, and other Cornell and Ithaca, New York subjects and scenes. Photographs, ca. 1960-1980; 35 mm. black and white transparencies of historical campus photographs; 2 x 2 black and white and color slides, some identified; 35 mm. and 2 x 2 negatives, with guide; and contact sheets of prints and negatives; 8 x 10 photographs of the Theory Center; 35 mm. slides of Roberts Hall; 35 mm. slides of Maplewood Park. Photographers included: Sol Goldberg, Russ Hamilton, and Charlie Harrington.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also Cornell University Office of Visual Services Protest Photographs, #4-3-2093.
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
The job lists indicate the date and subject of photography assignments. Assignment numbers consist of year, month, and day. Example: 7043=April 3, 1970. During the mid 1980's there was a slight change to the numbering system. During these years a leading A was used to replace the number 8. Example: A40801=August 1, 1984. Halfway through 1985, the numbering system returned to its original form. The assignment numbers refer to both the negatives and corresponding contact sheets. The contact sheets should be consulted first. If no contact sheet is in the box indicated then the negative rolls from the file cabinets can be used.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Assignments
Boxes 14-17
Series II. Slides
Boxes 1-13
Cite As:
